This year's theme is "The Call of the Open Sea". The event will span two and a half months, from the 17th June to the 31st August, transforming Tangier into an open air creative space in which private galleries, museums, and public spaces come together as one big work of art.

title: Oil falls to 3-1/2-month low after US, Iran sign ceasefire deal

BEIJING/SINGAPORE: Oil prices ​fell by more than $2 per barrel on Thursday after the US and Iran signed an interim agreement that ‌would end the Iran war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and waive US sanctions on Tehran’s oil, boosting the oil supply outlook.

	Brent crude futures were down $2.14, or 2.69 percent, at $77.41 a barrel as of 09:16 a.m. Saudi time, and US West Texas Intermediate fell $2.36, or 3.07 percent, to $74.43 a barrel.

Two years after the beginning of the modifications, the first U.S. Air Force F-16 equipped with DARPA’s Viper Experimentation and Next-gen Operations Model (VENOM) Autonomy Kit (VAK) []

title: 2 US troops killed, 1 missing after Iranian missile, drone attack in Jordan
description: Two U.S. service members were killed in action on Friday in an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack on U.S. and partner forces in Jordan.

title: Senegalese president assumes ECOWAS leadership
description: In tonight's edition, the Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye takes the helm of ECOWAS. Also, in Nigeria nearly 19 million Nigerian children are out of school, with the threat of kidnapping keeping many classrooms empty. And Tangier is celebrating the art of visual storytelling with the International Photography Festival.

title: Two French diplomats subjected to ‘extremely serious act of intimidation’ in Iran, FM Barrot says
description: France’s Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Monday said two French embassy officials had been subject to an “extremely serious act of intimidation” by Iranian security services in Iran the day before, having been held and interrogated for hours. One of them was also subjected to physical abuse, he said.

title: 'That is our challenge: to make politics work': Andy Burnham becomes UK PM
description: Andy Burnham has become Britain's new prime minister. The nomination process unfolded on July 20th in London, with one outgoing Labour leader passing the baton to another. The hope is that Burnham will ease the leadership turnover, stabilising the country.

title: Fears over Gulf shipping and water rise as US-Iran trade strikes
description: The United States and Iran have continued to exchange fire on July 20th, raising fears for Gulf shipping and water supplies in the region. After Kuwait reported Iranian attacks on water and electricity plants as well as oil facilities over the weekend, Bahrain said Iran this Monday, July 20th attacked civil air navigation systems. Despite this, Tehran says communication with Washington remains active through diplomatic channels.

title: Who is Khalil al-Hayya, Hamas’s new leader?
description: Hamas on Monday named Khalil al-Hayya, one of the founding members of the Palestinian militant group, as its new leader. Hayya is seen as a respected and pragmatic Hamas veteran “who doesn’t get carried away easily”. Last year, he survived an Israeli attack in Doha targeting the Hamas leadership which left several people dead, including one of his sons.

Andy Burnham inherits the Labour leadership at a moment when Britain's political debate is defined less by ideology than by structural constraints. title: Haute Couture Fall-Winter 2027: Big on spectacle and on message

title: US strikes Iran for ninth consecutive day as more ships targeted in Strait of Hormuz
description: The United States carried out a new wave of strikes in Iran on Monday as US deaths in the renewed fighting climbed to three and more ships came under attack in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Iran responded by targeting US military assets in Bahrain, the home of the US Navy's 5th Fleet, and Kuwait.

title: India's Cockroach party leads thousands of youth in protest to parliament
description:
Demonstrators in the Indian capital have marched to parliament as they demand the sacking of the country's education minister.
 
The youth-led Cockroach movement, started as a satirical youth platform, but has morphed into something larger.
 
Protesters are more galvanised after a hunger-striking social activist was forcibly moved to hospital, against his will.
